bodyText.en-IN: Underlying RC profit for the quarter was $2.4 billion, compared with $1.4 billion for the previous quarter. Compared with the first quarter 2025, the underlying result reflects an average gas marketing and trading result, stronger realized refining margins, stronger customers result, a strong oil trading result, partly offset by lower liquids and gas realizations and significantly higher level of refinery turnaround activity. The underlying effective tax rate (ETR)* in the quarter was 36%, compared with 50% for the previous quarter, which reflects changes in the geographical mix of profits.-IN
bodyText.en: Underlying RC profit for the quarter was $2.4 billion, compared with $1.4 billion for the previous quarter. Compared with the first quarter 2025, the underlying result reflects an average gas marketing and trading result, stronger realized refining margins, stronger customers result, a strong oil trading result, partly offset by lower liquids and gas realizations and significantly higher level of refinery turnaround activity. The underlying effective tax rate (ETR)* in the quarter was 36%, compared with 50% for the previous quarter, which reflects changes in the geographical mix of profits.

content.value: Castrol motor oil protects an engine in many ways, including prevention of the formation of deposits on engine valves and pistons. These deposits often occur as a result of the extreme internal operating temperatures created during the fuel combustion process. Castrol’s high-quality motor oils clean these critical metal surfaces with detergent additives to prevent deposits from forming even under intense operating conditions.​

Castrol motor oil protects an engine in many ways, including prevention of the formation of deposits on engine valves and pistons. These deposits often occur as a result of the extreme internal operating temperatures created during the fuel combustion process. Castrol’s high-quality motor oils clean these critical metal surfaces with detergent additives to prevent deposits from forming even under intense operating conditions.​

title.en-IN: Video | How does engine cleanliness maximize performance?-IN

content.value: Viscosity breakdown in motor oil can result from the mechanical shear that moving engine parts put on the lubricant. This shear breaks the oil down at the molecular level and reduces the initial viscosity. This reduction in viscosity increases the probability of metal to metal contact and decreases the motor oil’s ability to provide engine protection.

Viscosity breakdown in motor oil can result from the mechanical shear that moving engine parts put on the lubricant. This shear breaks the oil down at the molecular level and reduces the initial viscosity. This reduction in viscosity increases the probability of metal to metal contact and decreases the motor oil’s ability to provide engine protection.

content.value: Here at Castrol, we understand the importance of looking after the environment by recycling used motor oil. In fact, Castrol group companies are already working on ways to make sure that every ounce of used motor oil is recycled and re-refined, as you can see at www.nexcel.co.uk

You can play your part by making sure that you dispose of your old motor oil responsibly. The simplest way to do this is to put it inside the container your new oil came in, and either return it to the retailer you bought it from, or take it to your nearest auto workshop.
If neither of these options is available, you can take your used motor oil and filter to your nearest recycling location, which you can find via the

You can play your part by making sure that you dispose of your old motor oil responsibly. The simplest way to do this is to put it inside the container your new oil came in, and either return it to the retailer you bought it from, or take it to your nearest auto workshop.

content.value: P Lead (Intro Paragraph): Modern infrastructure is not defined solely by scale, but by the systems that support clarity, resilience, and long-term performance. This template demonstrates how structured content, consistent spacing, and intentional hierarchy enable authors to communicate complex ideas with confidence. Whether used for thought leadership, technical documentation, or operational reporting, this layout ensures that narrative and structure work together rather than compete for attention.
Clear hierarchy transforms dense information into something readable, navigable, and actionable — especially when multiple contributors are involved.
Heading (H2): Building the Foundation of Modern Infrastructure

Every large-scale initiative begins long before physical construction or deployment. It starts with cross-functional planning, regulatory alignment, sourcing strategies, and system-level design decisions that influence long-term operational success.

Structured documentation frameworks — such as clearly defined design systems or operational playbooks — help organisations scale without introducing unnecessary complexity. When hierarchy is predictable, readers can focus on insight rather than structure.
Sub-Heading (H3): Precision in Materials and Process
Material selection plays a crucial role in overall system performance. Structural components must balance durability, cost efficiency, and environmental responsibility.
further enhances performance. Standardised workflows, quality controls, and traceable documentation create consistency across teams and regions.
Sub-Heading (H4): Supporting Systems and Secondary Processes
Behind every primary operation exists a network of supporting systems. These
may be less visible but are equally critical in maintaining performance and safety standards.
It has survived not only rapid technological shifts, but also evolving regulatory frameworks that demand transparency and accountability.
Sub-Heading (H2): Optimising Performance Across Operational Lifecycles
H3: Grumpy wizards make a toxic brew for the jovial queen.
Sub-Heading (H5): Risk Management and Contingency Planning
Sustained performance is not achieved through isolated improvements but through coordinated system refinement. From early-stage planning to
that decisions made today continue delivering
. Structured frameworks allow teams to evaluate performance holistically rather than in silos.
KPIs should be specific, measurable, and aligned with long-term objectives. Overloading dashboards with excessive metrics often obscures meaningful insight.
Clarity at this level prevents misinterpretation and ensures teams remain aligned on priorities.
PLead (H6): Escalation Protocols and Incident Response

Clear escalation pathways reduce decision latency during critical events. Teams must understand not only what actions to take, but when and how to communicate those actions across departments. Structured response reduces uncertainty and strengthens organisational resilience.
Continuous learning ensures that operational maturity increases over time.
